column2: Exactly as the class should have been. Professor Wu obviously cares about his students success and I feel as though he did everything possible to set us up for success. I don't know if this will come across the way I intend it to but I really appreciate how normal this class was. What I mean is that it didn't consume too much of my time or attention but I still came out having learned everything I needed to; it was a very seamless experience. I feel as though I got exactly what I wanted out of this class (a stress–free experience learning the basics of chemistry) and had I wanted something else (say, an introduction to the marvels of chemistry) Wu could have provided that too. Thank you professor Wu and keep up the good work <3

column2: Eric Wu is a HUGE step up from the last professor who taught chemistry. He is a phenomenal educator who focuses mostly on the logical and understanding parts of chemistry. Taking out most of the memorizing parts, it allows students to fully immerse into thinking how chemistry works. Afterall, it is how the exams, homework, and discussions are structured, solving through deconstruction and reconstruction of the problem. The lectures themselves explains thoroughly through the steps of each step, calculations, or concept. Lectures are beautifully structured with multiple options to engage such as asking questions during the lecture through a QR code, provided downloadable notes, office hours, and plenty more.

column2: One of Wu's greatest strengths is his lecture–filled notes. If we are already familiar with the course material, we can just follow the lecture with the notes without having to write down anything. It is also a great tool for reviewing before, say, a midterm or  the final exam. Another great strength of his is his coffee hours that he has with students after class. It's nice to get to know your teacher personally and establish a great connection. His final strength is the review sessions he has before midterms. They are well–organized and go over a good chunk of the material. The only weakness I can think of with Wu is how fast he goes during his lectures. I wish he could slow down a bit so I could retain the information more and process it more efficiently. However, this is only a small issue and can be fixed by just looking at his lecture notes after class.

column2: Eric explains concepts very clearly with an emphasis on intuition and he encourages students to understand the material being presented rather solely on rote memory. His enthusiasm for chemistry is contagious. He is extremely welcoming, knowledgeable, approachable, and responsive to questions, and he takes the time to make sure students understand what is going on. The course is easy to navigate and students are not limited by access to learning resources (e.g., lecture recordings, lecture notes, practice problems/exams). Before taking course, I did not feel that I had a solid foundation in chemistry from previous classes. Eric’s class truly helped me to patch those areas of insufficient knowledge and to develop a greater understanding and enthusiasm for chemistry. I’d highly recommend this class to other students. I think this class is one of the few classes I’ve taken at UCLA with such a strong emphasis on intuition and a deep understanding of the material. Having taken this class, I’m excited for future chemistry classes.

column2: Professor Wu has a teaching style I think is really helpful for me, especially as a chemistry major. I enjoy the filling out of a worksheet rather than slide shows because I can learn and understand the material at a better pace. I find his lectures to be very helpful because all the content on them is solely necessary to understand the concepts. Whether it is the definitions, conditions of certain things, diagrams, and also examples. Unlike other classes, I found it easier to follow his lectures even if I didn't have prior knowledge on the subject and even if I didn't I can always look back on them as a summary. Because of the helpfulness of the notes, I found the midterms to be quite fair. There's nothing too difficult or too abstract on the exams that I haven't learned before. I find the practice material and the homework to be very helpful in preparing for the exams. Additionally, during lectures, he mentions the type of work that needs to be shown on exams to get full points. He also provides solutions with steps which is where I think I learn the most from. I make a lot of simple mistakes but reviewing the actual steps needed to solve problems helps me solidify the things I should do on the exam. Though I have never asked a question during a lecture, I find the QR code on the worksheets where people can ask questions to be very smart as I know it can be intimidating for a lot of people to ask a question in such a big lecture hall. He is also approachable when it comes to asking him questions or asking things about himself during coffee hour. Overall, I feel like I learned a lot in this class.

column2: Dr. Wu genuinely cares for his students and wants us to succeed. He provides so many materials to learn and study from, whether in–class practice problems, zoom review sessions, concept worksheets, discussion section handouts, practice midterms, answer keys, short online problem sets, and even optional practice problems from the textbook. He also did coffee hours and office hours three times per week each and made an effort to get to know his students by name. I was worried about taking this course as someone who never took AP Chemistry in High School, but his structured slides, notes, and lectures made me feel like I could succeed and learn well. The pace of the class is definitely fast and there is a lot of information, but that just comes down to the quarter system and the scope of the class. I am so grateful to have had Dr. Wu and he has helped me to appreciate chemistry more.

column2: Eric is such a nice and empathetic person. He truly is such a nice professor who actually cares about us and it's apparent in everything he does. He also does a great job at explaining difficult concepts and as someone who has taken AP chemistry, I can say that he explains everything far more logically than I've ever learned before. In terms of improving, I feel like maybe he could cut back a bit of the content he presents in lectures. I think he's a great lecturer and there's a lot of material to cover in just 10 weeks but sometimes it feels a bit overwhelming and some of the content feels a little arbitrary. I think the exams are also pretty fair but for midterm 2, I felt like some of the questions had to be based off of guessing rather than facts we already knew. I understand that it's an opportunity to use what we've learned to solve new issues but I feel like it's more fair to cover questions formatted in a way that's more familiar and doesn't require as much guessing to answer. But overall, Professor Wu has been great and I have thoroughly enjoyed taking his class and really hope I can stay connected or take another class of his in the future.

column2:
Prof Wu is an excellent lecturer and instructor. He is well organized and clearly makes student learning a high priority. Wu consistently provides additional practice material and holds hours of review sessions to prepare us for our exams. It has also been a pleasure to attend Coffee Hours that he hosts in between his lectures, where we gain the opportunity to discuss various topics related and unrelated to his work and experience in the field of Chemistry. His grading is fair, the content of his lectures educational, and students always feel welcome seeking help.
A weakness of this course (rather than instructor) is that it is a bit too fast–paced and it is clear that 3 quarters of content have been condensed into two. I have personally struggled to keep up at times despite Eric Wu’s availability to help.
Overall, this course is a bit tougher than I’m used to but Eric Wu is an excellent lecturer across the board!

column2: The strengths of Eric is that he is very organized and very student oriented, meaning that he really cares about his students and their learning. As a result, the course has a lot of resources in the Canvas that are organized and posted in a timely manner for students to use for studying. Eric also hosts review sessions for upcoming exams which once again shows his commitment to his students. I thought that the combination between these two aspects of the course made it very enjoyable since it is rare for an instructor to be so attentive and timely to the organization of their Canvas and it makes it a much more enjoyable experience for students. Another detail that adds to Eric's dedication to his students is that he made accommodations for his students that were also taking LS 7A, since this course's final had the same date and time as the LS 7A final for this quarter, and he made sure to have an alternative final time and set it up before the course even began. Eric also posts lecture notes that are filled out and not filled, which accommodates to students' different learning styles, and he implemented a Google Form linked on the notes using a QR code for students to submit their questions during lecture, which also helps students since some students may be too shy to ask questions in lecture by raising their hand. This implementation showed that he cares and listens to students' feedback that was given through the mid–quarter feedback form. A weakness of the course would probably be that sometimes the material would be rushed during the end of lecture since Eric would be running out of time, since there is a lot of content to cover in the class.

column6: Says material would be rushed during end of lecture
